prose:
Frontier-orbital analysis of furan computed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard'), density fitting on by default, in PySCF. Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz (9 atoms: O, 4xC, 4xH) used as-is for a single point — not re-optimized at DFT. Gas phase (no implicit solvation). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell RKS). SCF converged in 9 cycles.

Headline result — HOMO-LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV. HOMO (index 17) = -6.3597638 eV; LUMO (index 18) = -0.1024036 eV. This is a Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy. Total electronic energy = -230.11429 Hartree (-6261.7289 eV). Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 6.3597638 eV, vertical EA = 0.1024036 eV, electronegativity = 3.2310837 eV, chemical hardness = 3.1286801 eV, chemical softness = 0.3196236 /eV, electrophilicity index = 1.6684195 eV.

Integrity: engine reports trustworthy=true, status 'ok'; all integrity checks passed (energy_finite ok, scf_converged ok, schema_header ok, schema_headline ok). Warnings: the result JSON contained no warnings — clean run, no warnings reported.

Reproducibility — command: cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz ; live JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpyf1q5cld/live.json. No experimental/literature comparison offered.
